mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the consumer price index signals mounting inflationary pressure that compresses real earnings expectations and raises the probability of future monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 55% during periods of rising CPIAUCSL, or the mean 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when CPIAUCSL is increasing.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 7.5% indicates substantial slack in the labor market that depresses wage income and consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 7.5%, or the mean return conditional on this trigger is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and increases in unemployment generate a demand-labor double-hit that amplifies downside risk for corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal does not outperform its single-proxy components in predicting negative 20-day returns, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression has a t-statistic below 2.0 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1%, rising industrial production signals a liquidity-fueled expansion that boosts corporate profitability and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1%, positive changes in INDPRO fail to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding corporate output and operating leverage that translates into higher expected earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units signal a collapse in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when HOUST is below 600, or the mean conditional return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with falling payroll employment creates a stagflationary credit crunch where tighter liquidity meets weakening labor income, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases over prior 3 months and PAYEMS decreases over prior 3 months
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to produce significantly worse 60-day returns than either single-proxy signal alone, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (t-statistic < 2.0 in walk-forward regressions).

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly GDP growth predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days only when the unemployment rate exceeds 6%, distinguishing a confirmed recession from a transient technical contraction.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6%, negative GDPC1 growth does not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or the directional accuracy of the signal falls below 55% walk-forward.
